content.rendered:
“Sinaloa Cowboys” isn’t a song I’d expect anyone to cover, but Cracker took a, um, crack at it back in 2003 on their Countrysides album. It’s a little countrified, but it still gets Bruce’s quiet, tragic story across quite nicely.

 

© April 2, 2018

excerpt.rendered: Cracker delivers a restrained country arrangement of Bruce’s dark tale, “Sinaloa Cowboys.”

jetpack-related-posts.title: Sinaloa Cowboys
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Inspired by actual headlines and a personal story, Bruce crafted the quiet, tragic tale of the Rosales Brothers.

jetpack-related-posts.title: October 28
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce's Bridge School Benefit sequel, the E Street Band's first-ever show, the DVD release that never was, and more--all from this date.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Introduction
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce Springsteen's eleventh studio album is one of his best-sounding and most thematically cohesive. It wears its influences on its sleeves, and we'll explore them inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice: Evacuation of the West
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Part spaghetti western, part Mad Max, part War of the Worlds, "Evacuation of the West" is a brilliant, cinematic, fully realized E Street Band outtake that cries out for an official release.

jetpack-related-posts.title: The Line
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: "The Line" is Bruce at his most cinematic and empathetic, a masterpiece of taut storytelling written years before immigration reform dominated the national discourse.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Balboa Park
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: A heartbreaking L.A. Times article inspires one of Bruce's most harrowing narratives--the tale of young lost boys in Balboa Park.
